headline: Sarawak Peatland Drainage Covers 1.2 Million Hectares
context: Wetlands International's 2025 mapping project found 1.2 million hectares of Sarawak's peatlands have been drained for agriculture, representing 52% of the state's total peat area.

cards.big: Peatlands store 28 times more carbon per hectare than tropical forests.
cards.sub: Sarawak has drained 52% of its peatlands — releasing stored carbon accumulated over 5,000 years.
At current rates, 180,000 hectares of agricultural peatland will sink below sea level by 2050.

cards.big: Oil palm on peat generates 30% lower yields than on mineral soil.
cards.sub: Long-term productivity decline makes peat agriculture economically irrational within 25 years.

cards.big: Indonesia's peatland moratorium covers 7.8 million hectares.
cards.sub: Sarawak has no equivalent moratorium. New drainage permits were issued for 34,000 hectares in 2024.

cards.big: Farming land that is sinking below sea level is not agriculture. It is a countdown.
Peatland drainage is a bet against physics. The question is not whether to stop but how to transition the 150,000 workers who currently depend on peat-based agriculture.
